ICC Reliability Summary

Test-Retest Reliability
ICC(2,k) - Two-way random, average measures
Direction ICC(2,k) 95% CI p
Left to Right 0.90 [0.76, 0.96] <.001
Left to Left 0.87 [0.67, 0.95] <.001
Right to Left 0.91 [0.78, 0.96] <.001
Right to Right 0.91 [0.77, 0.96] <.001

Paired T-Tests (Day 1 vs Day 2)

Systematic Bias Check
Paired t-tests: Day 1 vs Day 2
Direction Mean Diff (cm) t df p 95% CI
Left to Right -1.3 -0.40 19 0.691 [-8.2, 5.6]
Left to Left -7.7 -2.30 19 0.033 [-14.6, -0.7]
Right to Left -2.0 -0.63 19 0.534 [-8.5, 4.5]
Right to Right -5.9 -1.84 19 0.082 [-12.5, 0.8]

Day 1 vs Day 2 Comparison

Interpretation

ICC values indicate reliability strength:

ICC Interpretation
< 0.50 Poor
0.50 – 0.75 Moderate
0.75 – 0.90 Good
> 0.90 Excellent

All hop directions showed good to excellent test-retest reliability (ICC range: 0.84 – 0.91). Non-significant paired t-tests indicate no systematic bias between testing days.
